Event: Takes over company as acting CEO after Logan’s stroke
Explanation: Gains power and keeps company running despite later risks.

Event: Struts around office emulating Logan as CEO
Explanation: Inflates ego while degrading leadership quality and stability.

Event: Partners with Stewy, putting company at risk
Explanation: Gains short‑term leverage but endangers company long‑term.

Event: Exposes cruises scandal publicly
Explanation: Boosts his own position/moral image while severely harming company and colleagues.

Event: Throws wild celebratory parties after exposure
Explanation: Self‑destructive partying mildly benefits hangers‑on.

Event: Ignores legal team’s advice
Explanation: Undermines his own case and adds risk for everyone.

Event: Makes PR attempts to paint himself as social‑justice martyr
Explanation: Chases image with little gain while worsening credibility and case optics.

Event: Reverts to arrogant “season 1” self after believing he has CEO role
Explanation: Directly sabotages his own near‑certain CEO win and alienates others.

Event: Shiv votes against him for CEO
Explanation: Protects herself/company from his instability at heavy cost to him.

Event: Spends majority of time as strung‑out, suicidal kleptomaniac
Explanation: Severe self‑harm with incidental benefits to people he gifts/stole for.

Event: Shits his own bed after bender
Explanation: Pure self‑harm with only trivial impact on others.

Event: Mocks Connor for shitting on camping trip
Explanation: Entertains himself/others at Connor’s expense.

Event: Frequently apologizes to Logan when cowed
Explanation: Reinforces his own subservience while marginally placating Logan.

Event: Attempts suicide / contemplates repeatedly
Explanation: Harms himself; others only marginally “benefit” via reduced burden.

Event: Uses addiction and self‑destruction after Humiliation Conga
Explanation: Ongoing self‑harm with limited external fallout.

Event: Works behind Logan’s back to oust him
Explanation: If successful he’d gain power while harming father’s position.

Event: Drunk‑drives with waiter and crashes into lake
Explanation: Endangers self and kills another with no net benefit.

Event: Tries multiple times to dive to save waiter
Explanation: Risks self to attempt saving another.

Event: Flees crash scene to avoid responsibility
Explanation: Protects himself legally while deeply harming justice for victim.

Event: Accepts Logan’s blackmail and becomes subservient
Explanation: Saves himself from prison but destroys autonomy and mental health.

Event: Agrees to be “blood sacrifice” for cruises scandal
Explanation: Offers himself as scapegoat, shielding company while ruining his own standing.

Event: Later refuses to be fall guy and betrays Logan on TV
Explanation: Reclaims agency/position while wrecking his father’s.

Event: Uses known weakness to collect info as Logan’s Dragon
Explanation: Advances his and Logan’s power while harming unwary talkers.

Event: Calls Lawrence, insults him to provoke negative story
Explanation: Gets the damaging story he wants while hurting company reputation.

Event: Cries when people are kind to him
Explanation: Emotional release and bonding with others who mean well.

Event: Has belated love epiphany about Rava
Explanation: Gains self‑knowledge but no material benefit; helps respect her autonomy.

Event: Pretends to be cool crusader in Season 3 while panicking
Explanation: Thin self‑image boost while misleading allies about his stability.

Event: Explodes when dismissed as “mentally disturbed”
Explanation: Regains some dignity but looks volatile and easier to manipulate.

Event: Attacks Roman over infertility insult
Explanation: Damages himself and Roman and wrecks sibling alliance.

Event: Plays “Rape Me” at Shiv’s press conference
Explanation: Humiliates his sister and his own cause with no gain.

Event: Yells at Logan for hitting Roman and comforts Roman
Explanation: Protects brother at some risk to his own standing.

Event: Yells at Logan for making Shiv cry
Explanation: Stands up for Shiv while risking father’s wrath.

Event: Scolds Tom for disrespecting Shiv and offers to “take him down”
Explanation: Backs his sister and potentially deters Tom at minor personal cost.

Event: Comforts Shiv after Logan’s death
Explanation: Provides support and deepens sibling bond with some self‑soothing too.

Event: Bonds with Naomi over shared drug issues
Explanation: Mutual emotional relief despite destructive context.

Event: Is repeatedly broken by Logan’s abuse and returns to him
Explanation: Perpetuates his own suffering while preserving Logan’s control.

Event: Orders closure of Vaulter to please Logan
Explanation: Buys limited favor from Logan while devastating many workers.

Event: Lets Logan’s mild affection make him hesitate on no‑confidence vote
Explanation: Sabotages his own strategic move to indulge need for love.

Event: Blackmails himself emotionally into martyr pose over cruises
Explanation: Continues self‑flagellation while modestly advancing victims’ exposure.

Event: Backs out of being true Atoner and defends his dad
Explanation: Protects himself/family while undercutting justice.

Event: Frequently causes drama to gain attention
Explanation: Satisfies attention craving while stressing and harming others.

Event: Goes off the wagon after failed no‑confidence vote
Explanation: Deeply harms himself; others only loosely affected.

Event: Steals items during klepto phase
Explanation: Briefly soothes himself while harming victims of theft.

Event: Takes drugs with dangerous meth head group
Explanation: Extreme self‑endangerment with negligible effect on others.

Event: Almost overdoses out of spite when Greg sent to babysit
Explanation: Risks killing himself to scare/hurt others slightly.

Event: Almost drowns himself in pool
Explanation: Suicide attempt solely harms him while lightly affecting observers.

Event: Contemplates jumping off office roof in Season 2
Explanation: Suicidal ideation harms him; minor burden on others.

Event: Attempts to leave company and asks Logan to let him go
Explanation: Tries to free himself from abuse, arguably healthier for both.

Event: Logan refusing to buy him out to retain control
Explanation: Retains power through Kendall’s suffering.

Event: Initially rats out Roman and Shiv in “Hunting”
Explanation: Slightly improves his standing with Logan by selling out siblings.

Event: Reports back when people speak against Logan to “hang themselves”
Explanation: Strengthens his utility to Logan while harming those who spoke freely.

Event: Allows Logan to use his son Iverson as power‑play piece
Explanation: Submits to Logan, failing to protect his child.

Event: Lashes out at Logan after Logan hits Iverson
Explanation: Risks relationship with father to defend his son.

Event: Insists kids shouldn’t be around Logan
Explanation: Protects children and asserts healthy boundary at some personal cost.

Event: Gives free cereal and small perks to lower employees
Explanation: Gains some goodwill while materially helping staff at low cost.

Event: Pays more attention to staff, sometimes to retraumatize himself
Explanation: Feeds his guilt but also humanizes workers and aids relations.

Event: Ruins Dust by branding them coke addicts
Explanation: Torches their reputation over hurt pride with minimal gain.

Event: Orders Frank to spread that Dust girls are “junkies and sluts”
Explanation: Amplifies their harm without concrete benefit to himself.

Event: Takes theatre actor to Dundee, disrupting Connor/Willa’s play
Explanation: Satisfies his own whims and optics while sabotaging their production.

Event: Breaks up with Dundee girlfriend over saying “awesome”
Explanation: Frees himself from minor annoyance at cost to her and play.

Event: Tells Rava they should reunite because otherwise he’ll be unhappy
Explanation: Attempts to secure his comfort while dismissing her happiness.

Event: Cancels second cringe performance before it happens
Explanation: Spares himself and others from humiliation.

Event: Publicly calls out Logan on live TV in S2 finale
Explanation: Frees himself and gains moral capital while gravely injuring Logan and company.

Event: Demolishes Logan’s bathroom in tantrum, then cleans it
Explanation: Brief catharsis followed by subservience; little net benefit.

Event: Becomes Logan’s “number one enforcer” after blackmail
Explanation: Keeps status and safety while degrading his autonomy and hurting others for Logan.

Event: Gets close to snorting enough coke “so his heart would explode”
Explanation: Targets only himself with lethal intent.

Event: Chooses to burn Vaulter despite misgivings
Explanation: Buys Logan’s favor/discipline at huge cost to employees.

Event: Willingness to take fall for cruises as atonement for waiter
Explanation: Offers himself up, slightly easing his guilt and greatly protecting company.

Event: Secretly wants justice for cruises victims while seeking revenge
Explanation: Mixed motives still bring real exposure and potential redress.

Event: Confesses waiter killing to Shiv and Roman in S3 finale
Explanation: Harms his image but gains support and gives siblings truth.

Event: Later denies killing waiter in S4 to win CEO vote
Explanation: Undermines his own integrity and destroys sibling trust without gain.

Event: Gives strong Living+ investor presentation
Explanation: Improves his standing and company value.

Event: Delivers powerful off‑the‑cuff eulogy at Logan’s funeral
Explanation: Strengthens his stature and helps others process grief.

Event: Buys Pierce jointly with siblings
Explanation: All siblings gain a win and target gains a lucrative deal.

Event: Lets mania guide birthday party into humiliating spectacle
Explanation: Primarily self‑destructive with awkward impact on others.

Event: Asks Shiv to look after him if she becomes CEO
Explanation: Shows vulnerability and trust, strengthening sibling bond.

Event: Asks Shiv and Roman to back him as CEO in finale
Explanation: Attempts to secure power using familial leverage, destabilizing them.

Event: Strikes Roman during final confrontation
Explanation: Physical attack damages both brothers and their alliance.

Event: Pushes through grief to comfort Shiv and Roman after Logan’s death
Explanation: Uses his own pain to support and unify siblings.

Event: Contemplates suicide by river at series end
Explanation: Suicidal ideation solely harms him; siblings worry but are free of his power quest.
